title: 2-step addition word problems within 100
description: Practice solving two step word problems with addition. Numbers used are 100 or less.
topic_path: Get ready for patterns and problem solving

assessment_items.question_content:
Bre is reading a book. She read $12$ pages in the car, $16$ pages at the park, and then $68$ pages at home.

**How many pages did Bre read?**    

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] pages
hints:
Bre read $\tealE{12}$ pages in the car and then another $\goldE{16}$ pages at the park.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cb9c0edd5a8f2903cce67d2389987277ff680783)

$\tealE{12} + \goldE{16} = \maroonD{28}$

Bre read $\blueE{68}$ pages at home.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ca46df8e3c1fb330195fc6e4574f8f2b9b60103)

$\maroonD{28} + \blueE{68} = \purpleD{96}$

Bre read $\purpleD{96}$ pages.
Bre read a total of $\purpleD{96}$ pages.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lucky has a bag of gold coins. She hides $21$ gold coins in the backyard and $14$ gold coins in the kitchen.  Lucky still has $13$ coins to hide.   

**How many gold coins were in Lucky's bag at the start?**
    
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] gold coins
hints:
Lucky hid $\tealE{21}$ coins in the backyard and $\goldE{14}$ coins in the kitchen. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8fee6f31e8df77edb0c8b4926c0a5caa5a61cab0)

$\tealE{21} + \goldE{14} = \maroonD{35}$

Lucky still has $\blueE{13}$ coins to hide.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/53e13ce203730d381fba8684e3377d755a2ac835)

$\maroonD{35} + \blueE{13} = \purpleD{48}$

There were $\purpleD{48}$ total coins in her bag.
Lucky had $\purpleD{48}$ gold coins in her bag at the start.

assessment_items.question_content:
There are $29$ second graders at recess. Then, $27$ third graders and $43$ fourth graders join them at recess.

**How many students are at recess?**    

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] students
hints:
There are $\tealE{29}$ second grade students and then another $\goldE{27}$ third grade students outside at recess.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/536d0c83903ab2889939e8aea5b3dc9f9be5caa0)

$\tealE{29} + \goldE{27} = \maroonD{56}$

There were also $\blueE{43}$ fourth grade students at recess.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ab0e7c8d58bca112d951e080ca2ff21d200678c9)

$\maroonD{56} + \blueE{43} = \purpleD{99}$

There were $\purpleD{99}$ students at recess.
$\purpleD{99}$ students were outside at recess.

assessment_items.question_content:
Planet Punky has $14$ green aliens, $61$ red aliens, and $25$ blue aliens.  

**How many total aliens are on Planet Punky?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] aliens
hints:
Planet Punky has $\tealE{14}$ green aliens and $\maroonD{61}$ red aliens.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/24ebfb22f083235688e243a350cc7741ba76b7a0)

$\tealE{14} + \maroonD{61} = \goldE{75}$

Planet Plunky also had $\blueE{25}$ blue aliens.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cf5c555d552ebe826bd4f0e9bb5c4945fc8fbe74)

$\goldE{75} + \blueE{25} = \purpleD{100}$

There were $\purpleD{100}$ total aliens on Planet Plunky.
Planet Punky has $\purpleD{100}$ total aliens.

assessment_items.question_content:
At the school store, Budi bought $16$ pencils, $22$ erasers, and $14$ stickers.  

**How many total items did Budi buy at the school store?** 
 
[[☃ numeric-input 1]] items
hints:
Budi bought $\tealE{16}$ pencils and $\goldE{22}$ erasers.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b64337b5cd4f8ff543c5e2f47f1bf355ecccd9fe)

$\tealE{16} + \goldE{22} = \maroonD{38}$

Budi has $\blueE{14}$ stickers.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/86682258cac0f8bfe2b9f7f814d4f9b451fd0924)

$\maroonD{38} + \blueE{14} = \purpleD{52}$

There were $\purpleD{52}$ total items.
Budi bought $\purpleD{52}$ total items at the school store.

assessment_items.question_content:
Maria has $50$ Star stickers, $33$ Moon stickers, and $11$ Earth stickers.  

**How many total stickers does Maria have?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] stickers
hints:
Maria has $\tealE{50}$ star stickers and $\goldE{33}$ moon stickers.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bb8a3e23cb2212931551dea17d4a8999569d3f1a)

$\tealE{50} + \goldE{33} = \maroonD{83}$

Maria still has $\blueE{11}$ Earth stickers.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8821364753fb36ab0f3b01b0f04dc8200c0dfebc)

$\maroonD{83} + \blueE{11} = \purpleD{94}$

Maria had $\purpleD{94}$ total stickers.
Maria has $\purpleD{94}$ total stickers.

assessment_items.question_content:
A farmer plants $28$ trees in March, $27$ trees in April, and $40$ trees in May.  

**How many total trees did the farmer plant?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] trees
hints:
The tree farmer planted $\tealE{28}$ trees in March and $\goldE{27}$ trees in April.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9c56027b41e411e1c96c9e810d0ac3adbe8a837b)

$\tealE{28} + \goldE{27} = \maroonD{55}$

There were $\blueE{40}$ trees planted in May.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/bd9acf91b9b46d5e562bfb7c7d01fcbd14a318ec)

$\maroonD{55} + \blueE{40} = \purpleD{95}$

There were $\purpleD{95}$ total trees planted.
The farmer planted $\purpleD{95}$ trees.

assessment_items.question_content:
Joe started his math homework.  He finished $23$ problems by himself.  He finished $13$ more problems with help from Sal.  There are $9$ math problems left.    

**How many math problems did Joe have when he started?  **    

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] math problems
hints:
Joe completed $\tealE{23}$ problems by himself and $\goldE{13}$ problems with Sal.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9f6438f2e483e49d57db257c31191a77ab133b44)

$\tealE{23} + \goldE{13} = \maroonD{36}$

Joe completed $\maroonD{36}$ problems.

Joe has $\blueE9$ problems left to solve.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c89e214f8ea62f302949ddc44953ea501df8b346)

$\maroonD{36} + \blueE9 = \purpleD{45}$

There were $\purpleD{45}$ total problems.
Joe had $\purpleD{45}$ math problems when he started.

assessment_items.question_content:
At Camp Sunshine, $4$ kids went home sick.  Of the remaining campers, $18$ kids went hiking and the other $41$ kids spent the day swimming.  

**How many kids started the day at Camp Sunshine?**    

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kids
hints:
At camp, $\tealE{4}$ kids went home and $\goldE{18}$ kids went hiking.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/263a9022997cc12e4702b7fdb6be91e88a45c060)

$\tealE{4} + \goldE{18} = \maroonD{22}$

$\blueE{41}$ kids went swimming.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a2eea0c1479a6d56503cd3f49619d51ae7918b06)

$\maroonD{22} + \blueE{41} = \purpleD{63}$

There were $\purpleD{63}$ total kids at Camp Sunshine.
$\purpleD{63}$ kids started the day at Camp Sunshine.

assessment_items.question_content:
Dan brought $36$ vanilla cupcakes and $49$ chocolate cupcakes to his school bake sale. Then his friend brought $5$ more cupcakes.

**How many total cupcakes did the bake sale have to sell?**    

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cupcakes
hints:
Dan brought $\tealE{36}$ vanilla cupcakes and $\goldE{49}$ chocolate cupcakes

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1e215c773d59594fb83ba246e89f08485c3da00)

$\tealE{36} + \goldE{49} = \maroonD{85}$

Dan's friend brought $\blueE{5}$ more cupcakes.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/71eaeb1f5396b03b8b0457b93c1f399f65695598)

$\maroonD{85} + \blueE{5} = \purpleD{90}$

There were $\purpleD{90}$ total cupcakes.
The bake sale had $\purpleD{90}$ total cupcakes.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Lopez family is driving to the state fair. First, they drove $32$ kilometers to stop for a snack. Then, they drove another $35$ kilometers and stop for gas. There were $17$ kilometers left to drive.

**How many kilometers does the family have to drive to get to the state fair?**    

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kilometers
hints:
The Lopez family drove $\tealE{32}$ kilometers and then another $\goldE{35}$ kilometers.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7d69cff99b8ad42ea2770d4fd950601f4f7f659e)

$\tealE{32} + \goldE{35} = \maroonD{67}$

There were $\blueE{17}$ kilometers left to drive to the fair.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/01ab685c3a2ba11ed8bec0f5acb636afcef3c326)

$\maroonD{67} + \blueE{17} = \purpleD{87}$

They drove $\purpleD{84}$ kilometers.
The Lopez family drove $\purpleD{84}$ kilometers.

assessment_items.question_content:
During a camping trip, Ana ate $39$ grapes and Xan ate $22$ grapes. There were $14$ grapes left in the bag.

**How many grapes were in the bag before the trip?**    

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] grapes
hints:
Ana ate $\tealE{39}$ grapes and Xan ate $\goldE{22}$ grapes.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4be5fcfdc88da0479336a2b0c32396bd448a96e4)

$\tealE{39} + \goldE{22} = \maroonD{61}$

There were $\blueE{14}$ grapes left in the bag.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9e68bdcf2db29a25781915103ad4175aa032d790)

$\maroonD{61} + \blueE{14} = \purpleD{75}$

There were $\purpleD{75}$ total grapes.
The bag had $\purpleD{75}$ grapes before the trip.
